title: delegation — inbound + outbound webhooks
description: This module publishes NO webhooks. Rationale: delegation + impersonation are Academorix-internal concerns — there is no external integration party that consumes lifecycle events. External Slack + PagerDuty deliveries for impersonation-related notifications are owned by the notifications module + on-call transport respectively (declared there, not here). If a security-team SIEM eventually subscribes to the impersonation audit stream, that would be an outbound-webhook contract owned by compliance module (audit stream forwarding), not this module. This file exists for uniform module shape.

outbound.description: No outbound webhooks published from this module. The audit stream itself may be forwarded to an external SIEM in Wave 2, but that forwarding pipeline is owned by compliance module — the delegation module writes to HasAudit's audit stream + never fans out to external subscribers directly.

future: If a security-team SIEM (Splunk, DataDog Security, Panther) subscribes to the impersonation stream via webhook, the outbound contract lands in compliance/webhooks.json — this module remains the producer of the audit rows but not the forwarder.

If Academorix ever exposes the delegation lifecycle to tenant-side third-party integrations (e.g. a tenant's own workflow tool needs to react to OOF activation), an outbound webhook contract COULD land here in Wave 2. Would be scoped to tenant-plane events only (Created / Activated / Expired / RevokedEarly) — impersonation events are Academorix-internal and NEVER exposed to tenants.
